what_it_does: This ledger entry registers the openwebui-venv terrain: a 6.8G Python virtual environment at /home/tim/openwebui-venv whose bin/open-webui launches Open WebUI, an interactive chat/tools/vision/RAG playground on http://localhost:8080 that consumes the local vLLM chat and embedding servers.
observations: Launched manually via /home/tim/openwebui-venv/bin/open-webui, with no systemd unit and no Docker container
Depends on [[vllm-env]] for chat (:8000) and embed (:8001) backends
Relates to [[company]] through OPEN_WEBUI.md and open-webui-extras.md as a model-testing surface
Relation is recorded as connected, not external, because the expected systemd/Docker wire is absent
Launch binary mtime 2026-06-23 indicates recent manual use
standouts: Explicit verification that no systemd unit or Docker container launches this engine
Reclassification from assumed external engine to connected client of vLLM
Open WebUI positioned as the manual model-testing frontend for the Company
conventions: Follows the terrain-entry front-matter schema with type, name, register, aliases, path, relation, kind, status, dates, size, coverage, ports, depends-on, relates-to, and tags
Uses wiki-style cross-references such as [[vllm-env]] and [[company]]
Includes evidence commands and a correction note
Breaks the task's initial framing by updating the relation value rather than forcing an external classification
concerns: Manual/on-demand launch means lifecycle is not centrally managed by systemd or Docker
depends-on only names vllm-env; the exact upstream endpoint configuration is not captured
secrets: false, but environment-specific launch configuration was not inspected
notes: A manually launched Open WebUI venv on port 8080 that acts as the chat frontend for local vLLM backends, deliberately recorded as connected because it lacks systemd/Docker wiring.
questions: What exact command or environment variables are used to run bin/open-webui?
Is the venv started ad-hoc, from a shell alias, or triggered by some other mechanism?
Which Open WebUI version is installed?
How are the vLLM chat and embed endpoints configured (UI settings, env vars, or config file)?
Are custom tools, RAG pipelines, or user accounts configured in Open WebUI?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to describe the openwebui-venv terrain and its relationship to the Company; the content does that and explicitly corrects the assumption that it is an external systemd/Docker-wired engine.
apparent_themes: Manual/on-demand tooling coexisting with automated infrastructure
Separation of engine lifecycle management from frontend/client usage
intention_signals: Document that Open WebUI is a separate, manually operated chat frontend consuming the Company's vLLM model servers, and push back against the assumption that every engine must be systemd- or Docker-wired.
novelty: It is an engine entry whose primary distinguishing feature is that it is explicitly not launched by systemd or Docker, and the author uses the entry to correct the task's initial framing rather than accepting an incorrect external classification.
contribution: A clear, evidence-backed record of a manually launched Open WebUI venv, its port, its dependency on vLLM chat/embed servers, and the explicit distinction that it is a connected client rather than an externally wired engine.
summary_for_embedding: Terrain ledger entry for a 6.8G Open WebUI Python virtual environment at /home/tim/openwebui-venv launched manually via bin/open-webui on port 8080 and consuming the local vLLM chat and embedding servers as its model backends.
intention_for_embedding: To record Open WebUI as a manually operated, chat-based testing surface for the Company's local vLLM models and to correct its classification away from a systemd/Docker-managed external engine.
